title: NASA finalizes science plans for Artemis 2 lunar flyby
summary: NASA is finalizing science plans for the Artemis 2 lunar flyby mission, with astronaut observations to be completed by April 6. The mission aims to conduct detailed scientific observations during the lunar flyby.

title: Atlas 5 launches Amazon Leo satellites
summary: An Atlas 5 rocket successfully launched a new batch of satellites for Amazon's broadband constellation on April 4. The mission aims to speed up the deployment of Amazon's satellite network.

title: Space Force budget would more than double in Trump’s $1.5 trillion defense plan
summary: The U.S. Space Force's budget would more than double to $71 billion in fiscal year 2027 under a proposed $1.5 trillion defense plan by Trump, with over $60 billion allocated for procurement, research, and development.

title: Artemis 2 in good shape cruising towards the moon
summary: The Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft is performing well with only minor issues after its engine burn towards the moon. The mission is on track and progressing smoothly.

title: Italy’s Argotec plans to scale Florida satellite facility to meet rising US demand
summary: Argotec has opened its first U.S. satellite production facility near Kennedy Space Center in Florida to meet increasing demand from American space programs. The company plans to scale this facility to support its growth in the expanding U.S. space market.

title: White House again proposes steep NASA budget cuts
summary: The White House has proposed significant budget cuts for NASA for the second year in a row, which would heavily affect the agency's science programs and the International Space Station.

title: SpaceX and Amazon spar over satellite deployments
summary: Amazon plans to revise its broadband satellite constellation deployment plans following criticism from SpaceX regarding space safety risks. SpaceX had previously raised concerns about Amazon's deployment approach.

title: Europe’s strategic autonomy in space will define its role in the ‘second space age’
summary: Europe's future in space hinges on achieving strategic autonomy to avoid reliance on foreign technology in the 'second space age'. This autonomy is seen as crucial for Europe to maintain control and leadership in space activities.

title: China’s commercial Tianlong-3 rocket fails on debut launch
summary: China's commercial Tianlong-3 rocket failed during its debut launch due to an anomaly during ascent. The failure marks a setback for Space Pioneer's ambitions in the commercial space sector.

title: Artemis 2 heads to the moon
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission has launched toward the moon following a successful maneuver on April 2. This marks a significant step in the agency's lunar exploration program.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts — now halfway to the moon — report 'burning smell' from toilet, but everything's fine
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts reported a burning smell from their Orion spacecraft toilet, but Mission Control downplayed the issue. The crew is now over halfway to the moon.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 204 — A New NASA
summary: The 204th episode of the 'This Week In Space' podcast discusses the ongoing Artemis 2 mission from Houston. The episode features hosts Rod Pyle and Tariq Malik.

title: 'Avatar: Fire and Ash' is finally available to buy digitally, and we've got an exclusive behind-the-scenes clip
summary: The digital release of 'Avatar: Fire and Ash' is now available, featuring an exclusive behind-the-scenes clip with a notable quote about the Tsyong spirits.

title: Disclosure day: If ET made contact, how would we handle the news?
summary: Experts discuss potential reactions and protocols if humanity makes contact with an extraterrestrial civilization. The topic explores societal, scientific, and governmental responses to such a historic event.

title: Defender at 45: How an icon of space gaming is in danger of being lost to history
summary: The iconic arcade shooter 'Defender' turns 45 in 2026, raising concerns about its preservation for future generations. The game risks being lost to history without proper conservation efforts.

title: An Artemis 2 astronaut took a 'bath' on camera on the way to the moon. Mission Control's reaction was priceless (video)
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 pilot Victor Glover was caught on camera taking a 'bath' during a workout session, leading to a humorous reaction from Mission Control.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts are now headed to the moon. Why has it taken humanity so long to go back?
summary: The Artemis 2 mission marks humanity's first return to the moon since 1972, raising questions about the 50-year gap. The delay is attributed to shifts in space priorities, funding challenges, and technological advancements.

title: It's official: NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission will break humanity's all-time distance record
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission will break the all-time distance record from Earth, reaching 252,757 miles on April 6. This surpasses the previous record set by Apollo 13 in April 1970.

title: Astronauts on the ISS watched NASA's historic Artemis 2 launch from space
summary: Astronauts aboard the International Space Station observed NASA's historic Artemis 2 launch from space, marking a moment shared across Earth and low Earth orbit.

title: NASA Answers Your Most Pressing Artemis II Questions
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is underway with four astronauts flying aboard the Orion spacecraft around the Moon and back to test its deep space systems. The crew includes NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen.

title: Illuminated in Orion
summary: NASA astronaut Christina Koch and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen are seen inside the Orion crew capsule in a photo taken in April 2026. The image captures them in a dimly lit environment to avoid glare on equipment.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for Northrop Grumman’s CRS-24 Resupply Launch
summary: NASA, Northrop Grumman, and SpaceX are targeting April 8 for the CRS-24 resupply launch to the International Space Station. The Cygnus XL spacecraft, carrying 11,000 pounds of cargo, will launch aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket.

title: Meet NASA’s New Artemis II Science Officers
summary: NASA has appointed its first Artemis II science officers—Kelsey Young, Trevor Graff, and Angela Garcia—to integrate lunar science into Mission Control, shaping future crew observations and analysis on the Moon.

title: Virgil I. “Gus” Grissom
summary: The 100th anniversary of Virgil I. 'Gus' Grissom's birth is being marked. He was America's second astronaut to fly in space aboard the Liberty Bell 7 in 1961.

title: Hello, World
summary: NASA astronaut Reid Wiseman, commander of Artemis II, captured a stunning image of Earth from the Orion spacecraft, showcasing two auroras and zodiacal light during the translunar injection burn. This marks the first photos of Earth taken from the Orion spacecraft.

title: Barents Sea Tied to Low Arctic Sea Ice
summary: Patches of open water in the Barents Sea contributed to record-low Arctic sea ice extent in March 2026, matching the lowest maximum observed in satellite records.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Mission Leaves Earth Orbit for Flight around Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission successfully completed a translunar injection burn, marking the first time astronauts have left Earth orbit for a lunar flyby in over 50 years. The six-minute engine firing propelled the Orion spacecraft toward the Moon.

title: Artemis II Astronauts Launch to Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is set to launch in April 2026, sending astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen on a 10-day journey around the Moon and back aboard the Orion spacecraft.

title: Réunion Island Lava Reaches the Sea
summary: The February 2026 eruption at Piton de la Fournaise on Réunion Island has lasted longer and produced more lava than recent eruptions. The lava flow has now reached the sea.

title: Orion Spacecraft Races Toward Historic Lunar Flyby in Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is in its mid-flight phase as the Orion spacecraft approaches a historic lunar flyby. This marks a significant milestone for the mission, which aims to carry astronauts around the Moon.

title: Artemis II Orion performs TLI Burn — Now headed to the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully performed the Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn, marking a historic milestone over 50 years after Apollo. The spacecraft is now on its way to the Moon as part of the Artemis II mission.

title: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: Artemis II is set to return humanity to the Moon with a launch scheduled for Wednesday evening. The mission follows the historic Apollo 17 mission, which concluded in 1972.

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, involving rockets like SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz, with liftoffs planned from Florida, California, and Russia.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: America's Rocket Factory is contributing to NASA's Artemis mission, marking a significant milestone in the return of crewed launches beyond low Earth orbit. The collaboration highlights the growing role of private industry in space exploration.

title: SpaceX delivers 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit aboard the Transporter 16 mission from California. This marks another milestone in SpaceX's rideshare program.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon Base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Lunar Gateway. This marks a significant pivot in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider based in Ottobrunn, Germany, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket. The company is planning to attempt the launch again at a later date.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, targeting a 2028 launch. Administrator Jared Isaacman and other leaders revealed the plans for this significant mission.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is making significant progress on upgrades to its Starbase launch pads in preparation for the next generation of Block 3 Starships. This effort highlights the company's focus on increasing launch frequency.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry event or development, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A breakthrough silicon chip has been developed that integrates multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space electronics. This advancement could significantly enhance the capabilities of satellites and spacecraft.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team confirmed the spacecraft is operational and has 'phoned home'.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to discuss the implementation of decisions from the CM25 ministerial meeting, focusing on industrial opportunities and program priorities. These events aim to engage the industrial community and facilitate direct dialogue on follow-up actions.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is scheduled for launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will introduce ECOS version 6, a new costing software, on March 30, 2026, mandating its use by all suppliers in financial proposals for ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a football-sized, 3D-printed lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference while being cost-effective and simple to manufacture. This innovation addresses the growing risks of disruption in satellite navigation systems.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly on the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communications.

title: White House Requests $18.8B FY27 Budget for NASA
summary: The White House has proposed a $18.8 billion budget for NASA in fiscal year 2027, matching the previous year's request. The proposal includes cuts to NASA's science and STEM missions.

title: Insurance is Commercial Space Nuclear’s Biggest Headache
summary: Insurance is identified as the biggest challenge for commercial space nuclear missions, with experts calling it a 'show stopper'. The issue was highlighted during an industry seminar.

title: Charts Defining the Space Industry in Q1 2026
summary: The article discusses key trends and data points defining the space industry in Q1 2026, highlighting a resurgence in Earth Observation (EO) activities. It likely analyzes market growth, technological advancements, or policy shifts influencing the sector.

title: CLPS Companies Excited For NASA’s ‘Opportunity Bomb’ Lunar Plan
summary: NASA announced a plan for a lunar base supplied through monthly uncrewed lunar landings starting next year, exciting CLPS companies eager to participate. The initiative, dubbed an 'Opportunity Bomb,' aims to accelerate lunar exploration and infrastructure development.

title: Phantom Space Acquires Thermal Management Technologies
summary: Phantom Space has acquired Thermal Management Technologies, a company specializing in advanced satellite thermal components for in-space applications. This acquisition aims to enhance Phantom Space's capabilities in satellite thermal management systems.

title: Artemis II Is On The Way To The Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is en route to the Moon with a crew including the first woman and first person of color to head to the Moon, as well as the first non-American astronaut. The mission marks a historic step in space exploration.

title: Exclusive: In-Orbit Manufacturing Startup Dispatch Emerges from Stealth
summary: Dispatch, an in-orbit manufacturing startup, has emerged from stealth mode with $500,000 in funding to develop an uncrewed space station equipped with manufacturing infrastructure.

title: Antaris Raises $28M Series A
summary: Antaris has raised $28 million in a Series A funding round led by WestWave Capital, with additional investment from Lockheed Martin Ventures and other new investors.

title: Top Space Lawmaker on Moon Base, Artemis Plans
summary: A top space lawmaker expressed strong support for establishing a base on the Moon as a strategic step toward future Mars missions. The remarks highlight growing enthusiasm for NASA's Artemis program and lunar exploration efforts.

title: Voyager and Icarus Team Up on ISS Robotics Demo
summary: Voyager Space and Icarus Robotics are collaborating on a robotics demonstration aboard the International Space Station (ISS). This partnership highlights advancements in space robotics and automation.

title: Live in the Booth: Matt Desch Talks Iridium NTN Direct and Alt PNT Advances
summary: Iridium is launching Iridium NTN Direct, a standardized and global narrow-band satellite IoT service, led by CEO Matt Desch. This initiative positions Iridium as the first in this space.

title: Redwire Wins European Quantum Satellite Contract
summary: Redwire has won a contract to develop a quantum-secure satellite for the European Space Agency's Quantum Key Distribution Satellite (QKDSat) program. This initiative aims to enhance secure communications in Europe.

title: African Market for Satellite Services Offers Pent Up Demand
summary: Africa's large landmass of 11.7 million square miles presents challenges for satellite services, but demand is growing as of September 2025.

title: Amazon in Talks to Buy Globalstar, According to FT Report
summary: Amazon is reportedly in discussions to acquire Globalstar, according to a Financial Times report. The potential deal was first reported on Wednesday evening.

title: Gravitics Receives Strategic Funding Increase From SpaceWERX
summary: Gravitics secured a Strategic Funding Increase (STRATFI) contract from SpaceWERX to accelerate the development of its Orbital Carrier architecture. The funding aims to support the U.S. Space Force's innovation initiatives.

title: Astroscale and Exotrail to Co-Develop Servicing Mission
summary: Astroscale and Exotrail will collaborate on a satellite de-orbiting mission, combining Japanese and French expertise in on-orbit servicing to safely remove satellites from orbit.

title: In-Orbit Logistics Companies Call for Clearer Demand Signals from Gov
summary: In-orbit logistics companies are urging the government to provide clearer demand signals. The Space Development Agency (SDA) acknowledges the challenges of operating in space.

title: Canada’s Northwestel to Provide Broadband With Telesat Lightspeed Capacity
summary: Canada's Northwestel has signed a multi-year contract with Telesat to use capacity from the Lightspeed constellation for broadband services. This partnership will help Northwestel expand broadband access in remote regions like Nunavut and the Northwest Territories.

title: Phantom Space Acquires Component Supplier Thermal Management Technologies
summary: Phantom Space Corporation has acquired Thermal Management Technologies (TMT) to support the development of its orbital data center constellation and enhance its vertical integration strategy.

title: ArkEdge Space to Work With ABIT For IoT Service Rollout
summary: ArkEdge Space and ABIT Corporation have signed an MoU to jointly develop IoT solutions and satellite communication devices. The partnership aims to enhance satellite-based IoT services.
